The new Altera operating segment, which was previously reported under DCAI, follows its announced separation into a standalone business.
Intel Products targets 60% non-GAAP gross margin and 40% non-GAAP operating margin by the end of 2030.

Intel will also pause its fabrication efforts in Poland and Germany "by approximately two years based on anticipated market demand," Gelsinger said, and pull back on its plans for its Malaysian factory.

That division will now become an independent subsidiary of the company.","score":0.44622982,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theverge.com/2024/9/16/24246599/intel-foundry-independent-spinoff","title":"Intel’s big turnaround plan includes spinning off its chipmaking business | The Verge","content":"Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger plans to ‘establish Intel Foundry as an independent subsidiary.’. Intel is spinning off its chipmaking business as part of its plans to reverse billions in losses and a tumbling stock price. In an announcement on Monday, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger said the Intel Foundry will become an independent subsidiary with “clearer separation and independence” from Intel. Intel will also stop work on the factories it’s building in Poland and Germany for two years “based on anticipated market demand.” The company is still moving forward with its plants in Arizona, Oregon, New Mexico, and Ohio, however. As part of this announcement, Intel revealed that the Biden administration awarded the company up to $3 billion in funding to make chips for the US military. On top of widespread issues affecting 13th and 14th Gen CPUs, the company reported $1.6 billion in the first quarter of 2024, with its chipmaking business alone racking up $7 billion in operating losses in 2023.","score":0.40967003,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://wccftech.com/intel-ceo-future-plans-focusing-on-foundry-independent-subsidry-aggressive-restructuring-policies-ramping-up-x86-adoption","title":"Intel CEO Reveals Plans For The Future: Focusing on Foundry Now An Independent Subsidry, Aggressive Restructuring Policies & Ramping Up x86 Adoption","content":"Intel's CEO Pat Gelsinger and the board members have shared the outcomes of the latest shareholder meeting with its employees, providing an overview of what's next. There has been no shortage of rumors and speculation about the company, including last week’s Board of Directors meeting, so I’m writing today to provide some updates and outline what comes next. We had deep discussions about our strategy, our portfolio, and the immediate progress we are making against the plan we announced on August 1. The Board and I agreed that we have a lot of work ahead to drive greater efficiency, improve our profitability, and enhance our market competitiveness there are three key takeaways from last week’s meeting that I want to focus on:. To build on our progress, we plan to establish Intel Foundry as an independent subsidiary inside of Intel. A more focused and efficient Intel Foundry will further enhance collaboration with Intel Products.","score":0.39257023,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/corporate/foundry-momentum-progress-plan","title":"Pat Gelsinger on Foundry Momentum, Progress on Plan - Intel Newsroom","content":"Your source for all news Intel. # Pat Gelsinger on Foundry Momentum, Progress on Plan. ## A message from Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger to employees regarding the next phase of Intel's transformation. And we had deep discussions about our strategy, our portfolio and the immediate progress we are making against the plan we announced on August 1. The Board and I agreed that we have a lot of work ahead to drive greater efficiency, improve our profitability and enhance our market competitiveness—and there are three key takeaways from last week’s meeting that I want to focus on:. Specifically, Intel Foundry will produce an AI fabric chip for AWS on Intel 18A. This news, combined with our AWS announcement, demonstrates the continued progress we are making to build a world-class foundry business. To build on our progress, we plan to establish Intel Foundry as an independent subsidiary inside of Intel.","score":0.348934,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://intellionaire.substack.com/p/the-intellionaire-ep-24-unofficial","title":"The Intellionaire Ep. 24 - Unofficial Intel Foundry Direct Connect 2026","content":"# The Intellionaire Ep. 24 - Unofficial Intel Foundry Direct Connect 2026. I think all of us are probably a bit gutted that Intel cancelled the Intel Direct Connect 2026 which was supposed to be held last week. I have to say, I agree that this is the right approach. So, without an official Foundry Direct Connect this year, I thought I would write up my own unofficial summary of Intel Foundry in 2026. I will focus on the logic side of things this week, with advanced packaging to follow in the near future. Intel, however, is well placed to attract external customers with a solid, US-based R&D division, as well as the most US/EU capacity for advanced logic, combined with a now proven track record of implementing both GAA and BSPD simultaneously - something which no other Foundry in the world has done yet in a commercial process.","score":0.30775368,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.73,"request_id":"5f41d619-ba9f-4661-99d4-2aaeae7488b8"}
